brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: n is a count per minute. I want events per second. rate(n, 1m) converts it. Show me the top twenty by rate.

expected.sql: SELECT minute, service, ROUND(n / 60.0, 2) AS rps FROM metrics ORDER BY rps DESC LIMIT 20

hints.text: Carol: 'Too many rows. LIMIT 20 is already in the query.'
Carol: 'Not enough rows. The field should be n.'
Carol: 'Query error. The field inside rate() is n — just the column name, no quotes.'
Carol: 'Wrong values. The field is n — total events per minute.'
Carol: 'rate(n, 1m). n is the count column. 1m matches the bucket size.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: That's the unit I can think in. Most services running at fractions of a second. Some minutes running hotter.
